fact: November 20 is exactly 324 days into the year, which means there are only 41 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when squirrels go absolutely nuts burying their winter stash - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns and remember where most of them are!
The sun sets before 5 PM in most northern cities around this date, making it feel like bedtime when you're still doing homework!
November 20 falls smack in the middle of Scorpio season, when people born under this zodiac sign are supposed to be extra mysterious and intense.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, November 20 is when jacaranda trees explode with bright purple flowers that carpet the ground like nature's confetti!
This date is exactly one month before the winter solstice in the north, meaning daylight is shrinking by about 2-3 minutes every single day!
Deer in North America are in the middle of their mating season around November 20, which makes male deer act like total show-offs and fight each other with their antlers!
The moon phase on November 20 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it appears extra bright because the air is clearer and crisper in late autumn!
In northern forests, this is when most deciduous trees have dropped their leaves, creating a crunchy carpet that can be 6 inches deep!
November 20 is when many birds in cold regions have already finished their epic migrations - some Arctic terns have flown over 25,000 miles by this date!
The average temperature drop from November 1 to November 20 in temperate regions is about 10-15 degrees Fahrenheit, making your breath visible on most mornings!
This date marks the time when hibernating animals like bears have already found their winter dens and are getting seriously sleepy - some will sleep for the next 5-7 months!
